title: Pea soup – all year round
excerpt: Look, we don't wait for cold weather to eat soup. We eat it all year round. To us soup isn't a starter – it's a meal. I make it thick and hearty with meat in it. Then we feast on the soup with a lovely glass of communion wine …

seo.title: Pea soup - all year round - Marinda Kook

html:
Look, we don't wait for cold weather to eat soup.  We eat it all year round.  To us soup isn't a starter – it's a meal to us.  I make it thick and hearty with meat in it. Then we feast on the soup with a lovely glass of communion wine.

If you don't have meat to put in, you can use  2 litres of meat stock (the kind you buy) instead. You also don't have to put in the ham hock and the bacon, it just gives it a lovely smoky flavour.  If you really want to be fancy you can, when serving, add a swirl of cream on each portion or crumble feta cheese over it. You can bake this easy bread roll and serve it with your soup.

Enjoy the delicious pea soup and never ever again may you say you can't make pea soup.

items: 1 small smoked ham hock or 1 packet diced bacon (this is optional)
a little oil to fry the onions in

steps: Peel your vegetables and cut into small cubes.
Cut your onions into rings and fry them together with your meat until brown in a little oil in a nice big pot on the stove.
Now add the rest of the ingredients to the pot and cook until soft. Stir regularly.
If you use a pressure cooker it will take 60 minutes.  In a regular pot it will take longer.  You'll just have to feel until it's soft.
Now you must taste, taste, taste.  Add more salt, pepper or Worcestershire sauce to your liking.
Take the meat out and mash the vegetables or use a blender and blend the vegetables smooth.
Put the meat back into your pot and enjoy the pea soup.
